knowledge_components.name: Place value positions represent powers of 10, and multiplying or dividing by 10 changes the value of numbers

complicators.name: Identify place value positions and understand that each position represents a power of 10
Multiplying by ten increases the value of a number and dividing by ten decreases the value of a number. When doing so, the digits do not change, just their value.
Compare relative values of same digits in different positions using multiplicative relationships
Fluently manipulate, utilize, and compare numbers based on an understanding of place value.

knowledge_components.name: Standard rounding rules help estimate and simplify computations

complicators.name: Use rounding strategically to build number sense, judge reasonableness of answers, and estimate results of calculations (single step)
Apply standard rounding rules for whole numbers and decimals to specified place values and determine which place value is best to use
Understand the impact of rounding on computational accuracy and make directional predictions. Estimate results of single-step and multi-step calculations.

knowledge_components.name: The properties of addition and multiplication form the foundation of arithmetic, revealing how numbers relate, combine, and can be manipulated with consistency

complicators.name: Apply identity properties with 0 and 1 in computational contexts
Use commutative and associative properties to reorganize calculations efficiently
Employ the inverse relationships between operations (without getting into negative numbers yet)
Apply inverse relationships and identity, commutative and associative properties with single variables to start practicing the generalizability of the properties and relationships.

knowledge_components.name: The distributive property shows how multiplication spreads across addition to break apart or combine expressions.

complicators.name: Practice the area model with partial products to see the distributive property work visually (up to three additive terms)
Practice the distributive property symbolically with problems that can use mental math
Apply the distributive property to simplify expressions or as a strategy for more manageable calculations.

knowledge_components.name: Exponents represent repeated multiplication of equal factors

complicators.name: Interpret exponent notation and evaluate simple powers with positive integer bases

Special cases:
- Consider basic fractions and decimals as bases, compared to their original or additive values.
- Powers of 0 and 1

knowledge_components.name: Order of operations ensures consistent computation results

complicators.name: Apply PEMDAS to expressions with parentheses and basic operations.
Apply PEMDAS to expressions with nested parentheses/brackets.
Simplify expressions that use a fraction bar as a grouping symbol
